meta.note: All stories are based on documented cultural sources. Wikipedia URLs reference real articles.

stories.title_en: Pangu Creates the World
stories.summary_en: Before the universe existed, there was only a vast cosmic egg of swirling chaos. Inside it, the giant Pangu slept for eighteen thousand years. When he woke, he swung a great axe, splitting the egg: the light rose to become Heaven, the heavy sank to become Earth. Fearing they would merge again, Pangu stood between them, pushing the sky higher with his hands and pressing the earth lower with his feet, growing ten feet taller each day. After another eighteen thousand years, he collapsed from exhaustion and died. His breath became the winds and clouds, his voice the rolling thunder, his left eye the sun and his right the moon. His four limbs became the mountains, his blood the rivers, his flesh the soil, his hair the forests, his sweat the rain, and the fleas on his skin became all of humanity.
stories.summary_zh: 宇宙初开之前，混沌一片，犹如一枚巨卵。盘古在其中沉睡一万八千年，醒来后以巨斧劈开混沌，轻清者上升为天，重浊者下沉为地。盘古恐天地重合，以手撑天，以足踏地，每日长高一丈，如此又过一万八千年。终因力竭倒下，化为万物：呼吸成风云，声音成雷霆，左眼成太阳，右眼成月亮，四肢化山岳，血液化江河，肌肤化土壤，毛发化林木，汗水化雨露，身上虮虱化为芸芸众生。

stories.title_en: Chang'e Flies to the Moon
stories.summary_en: The divine archer Hou Yi shot down nine of ten suns that scorched the earth, saving humanity. As reward, the Queen Mother of the West gave him a pill of immortality — enough for two. He hid it at home while he went hunting. His wife Chang'e discovered it and, fearing a villainous apprentice who sought to steal it, swallowed the whole pill herself. She immediately began to float upward, rising through the roof, past the clouds, and into the night sky until she landed on the moon. There she lives to this day in the Cold Palace, accompanied only by the Jade Rabbit who pounds elixirs. Hou Yi, grief-stricken, laid out her favorite foods every full moon night as an offering. This is said to be the origin of the Mid-Autumn Festival.
stories.summary_zh: 神射手后羿射落九日，拯救苍生，西王母赐予他长生不老药，足供两人服用。后羿外出狩猎，徒弟蓬蒙趁机入室欲盗仙药。嫦娥情急之下独吞仙药，随即身轻如燕，飞升而起，穿云破雾，径奔月宫。从此嫦娥居于广寒宫，唯有玉兔相伴，日夜捣药。后羿悲痛欲绝，每逢月圆，便摆出嫦娥最爱的食物遥祭，此举相传便是中秋节的起源。

stories.title_en: The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl
stories.summary_en: Zhinü, a goddess and celestial weaver, descended to earth to bathe in a lake. The humble cowherd Niulang, guided by his magical ox, hid her heavenly robe. Without it she could not fly home, and the two fell in love and married, living happily with two children. When the Jade Emperor discovered his granddaughter had married a mortal, the Queen Mother of the West scratched a river across the sky — the Milky Way — to separate them forever. The lovers wept so bitterly that magpies, moved by pity, flew up and formed a living bridge across the silver river once a year, on the seventh night of the seventh lunar month. On that night alone, Niulang and Zhinü can cross and embrace. This tale is the origin of the Qixi Festival, China's Valentine's Day.
stories.summary_zh: 织女下凡沐浴，牛郎在神牛指引下藏起天衣，二人因此相识相爱，成婚生子，过着幸福生活。玉帝得知后震怒，王母娘娘用金簪在天上划出一道银河，将二人永久分隔。牛郎织女泪洒成河，感动喜鹊，每年七月初七，万千喜鹊飞上天空，以身搭桥，让二人得以相会片刻。此传说即是七夕节的由来，被誉为中国情人节。

stories.title_en: Nüwa Repairs the Sky
stories.summary_en: In ancient times, the pillar holding up the sky collapsed when the water god Gonggong, defeated in battle, crashed his head against Mount Buzhou in rage. The sky cracked open, the earth tilted, floods poured through the breach, and fire broke out everywhere. Humanity faced extinction. The goddess Nüwa, who had once fashioned humans from yellow clay, could not bear to watch her creation perish. She gathered five-colored stones from riverbeds and melted them in a great furnace, using the molten stone to patch the gaping wounds in the sky. She killed a great tortoise and used its legs as new pillars to support the heavens. She slew the black dragon that was terrorizing central China and used the ashes of reeds to dam the floodwaters. The sky was sealed, the earth stabilized, and humanity was saved.
stories.summary_zh: 远古时代，水神共工战败，怒触不周山，擎天柱折断，天穹裂开，洪水四溢，烈火蔓延，人类濒临灭顶之灾。女娲不忍见自己以黄土捏成的人类就此消亡，遂采来五色石，以神火熔炼，将天穹缺口一一修补。她斩大龟，以其四足代替天柱撑起苍穹；斩杀为祸中原的黑龙；燃芦苇，以灰烬堵塞洪水。天补地平，人类得救，女娲之名永载史册。

stories.title_en: The Legend of the White Snake
stories.summary_en: Bai Suzhen, a thousand-year-old white snake spirit, took human form and fell deeply in love with a kind scholar named Xu Xian on the West Lake in Hangzhou. They married and opened a medicine shop, healing the poor. But the monk Fahai, believing snake demons could only bring ruin, relentlessly pursued them. He revealed Bai Suzhen's true nature to Xu Xian and imprisoned her beneath Leifeng Pagoda on the lakeshore. Their son grew up, passed the imperial examinations with honors, and returned to offer incense at the pagoda. His filial devotion weakened its ancient magic, and the pagoda collapsed, freeing his mother at last. The story is one of China's four great folk tales, a meditation on love, persecution, and the boundary between human and spirit.
stories.summary_zh: 修炼千年的白蛇精白素贞化为人形，在西湖邂逅书生许仙，二人情定终身，在杭州开设药铺，悬壶济世。法海和尚认定蛇妖必会为害，强行拆散鸳鸯，将白素贞镇压于雷峰塔下。多年后，白素贞之子许仕林学有所成，高中状元，回乡祭塔尽孝，母子相聚的深情终于撼动塔基，雷峰塔轰然倒塌，白素贞重获自由。此传说为中国四大民间故事之一，千百年来传唱不衰。

stories.title_en: Sun Wukong the Monkey King
stories.summary_en: Born from a sacred stone on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit, Sun Wukong became king of the monkeys and then studied under an immortal master, gaining 72 transformations, cloud-somersaulting, and a magical staff that could shrink to a needle or grow to pierce the sky. Unsatisfied, he raided the Dragon King's treasury, stole the Jade Emperor's peaches of immortality, and proclaimed himself the Great Sage Equal to Heaven. The celestial army could not subdue him. Finally, the Buddha himself challenged the Monkey King to escape his palm — Sun Wukong leaped to the ends of the universe only to discover he had never left the Buddha's hand. He was imprisoned beneath Five Elements Mountain for five hundred years, until the monk Xuanzang freed him to journey to India for the Buddhist scriptures.
stories.summary_zh: 孙悟空生于花果山一块仙石，拜师学艺，习得七十二变和筋斗云，更得定海神针如意金箍棒。他大闹龙宫，抢夺法宝；大闹天宫，偷吃蟠桃，自封「齐天大圣」，天兵天将奈何不得。最终如来佛祖以手掌为擂台，悟空跃至天涯海角，却始终未离佛掌。被压五行山下五百年后，他随唐僧西天取经，踏上漫漫征途，终成正果。

stories.title_en: Meng Jiangnu Weeps Down the Great Wall
stories.summary_en: During the reign of the first Qin Emperor, millions of men were conscripted to build the Great Wall. Meng Jiangnu's husband Fan Qiliang was taken away on their wedding night. She waited and waited, and when winter came she sewed warm clothes to bring him at the Wall. After a grueling journey across mountains and rivers, she arrived only to learn he had died of exhaustion and been buried within the Wall itself. She wept bitterly for three days and nights without stopping. Her grief was so profound and so pure that eight hundred li of the Great Wall crumbled before her tears, revealing her husband's bones. She gathered them, buried them properly, and chose death in the sea over the Emperor's cruel offer of mercy. Her story became a symbol of resistance against tyranny.
stories.summary_zh: 秦始皇修筑长城，征发天下劳役，孟姜女的丈夫范杞良在新婚之夜被强征服役。她苦苦等候，寒冬来临，亲手缝制寒衣，跋山涉水送往长城。然而抵达时，她得知丈夫已因劳累而死，尸骨被筑入城墙之中。她在长城脚下痛哭三天三夜，哭声震天，八百里长城为之崩裂，终现丈夫枯骨。她收殓遗骨，安葬后毅然投海殉情，拒绝了秦始皇的威逼利诱。此传说成为反抗暴政的千古象征。

stories.title_en: Hou Yi the Divine Archer
stories.summary_en: In the time of Emperor Yao, ten suns — the sons of the Jade Emperor — all rose together into the sky, scorching the earth, burning crops, drying rivers, and melting rocks. Humanity was on the brink of extinction. Emperor Yao pleaded with the divine archer Hou Yi to restore balance. Hou Yi strung his great red bow and shot down nine of the suns one by one, each falling as a golden crow. He would have shot the last as well, but Yao, knowing the world needed at least one sun, secretly removed one arrow from his quiver. The remaining sun, terrified, has kept regular hours ever since. For this heroic act Hou Yi was exiled from heaven — his bow and arrows were too dangerous to keep in the divine realm — and he lived out his days on earth as a mortal hero.
stories.summary_zh: 帝尧时代，天帝之子十日同时升空，炽烈阳光将大地烤焦，禾苗枯死，河流干涸，民不聊生。帝尧祈请神射手后羿解救苍生。后羿张开红弓，连射九日，九只金乌相继坠落。他本欲射落最后一日，帝尧却悄悄抽走一支箭矢，因天地不可无日。仅存的太阳自此心存敬畏，按时升落，不敢懈怠。后羿因其神弓威力过大，被天庭放逐，此后以凡人之身在人间度过余生，成为英雄传说中的不朽名字。

stories.title_en: The Dragon King of the Eastern Sea
stories.summary_en: The Dragon King Ao Guang ruled the Eastern Sea from a vast crystal palace beneath the waves, controlling rain, storms, rivers, and the tides. He possessed an arsenal of miraculous treasures, including the legendary Ruyi Jingu Bang — the pillar that once steadied the sea floor — which the Monkey King Sun Wukong famously stole. Dragon Kings govern the four seas of China and answer to the Jade Emperor above. When mortals faced drought, they would pray at dragon temples; when floods came, they would beg the Dragon King for mercy. He is simultaneously feared and revered — a divine bureaucrat of the waters, mediating between the human world and the heavens, capable of summoning typhoons or gentle rain with equal ease.
stories.summary_zh: 东海龙王敖广居于深海水晶宫殿之中，掌管东海风雨、江河潮汐，拥有无数奇珍异宝，其中包括镇海神针定海神珍铁——即后来被孙悟空强取的如意金箍棒。四海龙王各司其职，统辖四方海域，受玉皇大帝节制。民间逢旱求雨，必祭龙王庙；洪涝之时，百姓亦叩首祈求龙王收敛。龙王集威严与仁慈于一身，既是水界神灵，也是沟通人间与天庭的神圣使者。

stories.title_en: Nezha the Lotus Prince
stories.summary_en: Nezha was born after his mother carried him for three years and six months, finally giving birth to a flaming sphere from which the child emerged wearing a red armband and golden ring. From infancy he was wild and ungovernable, killing the Dragon King's son while bathing in the sea. To prevent his family from suffering the Dragon King's revenge, Nezha drew his sword and sliced off his own flesh, returning it to his parents, freeing them from obligation. His soul was later reborn by his master Taiyi Zhenren from lotus flowers and reeds, creating a new immortal body of pure lotus. Nezha, now a golden-ringed spirit child riding on Wind Fire Wheels with a Universe Ring in hand, became one of the most beloved child deities in Chinese religion, fierce, compassionate, and eternally young.
stories.summary_zh: 哪吒在母腹中孕育三年六个月，降世时以火球形态出世，出生便佩乾坤圈、混天绫。幼时顽劣，在海中嬉戏时打死龙王三太子，为免父母受龙王报复，哪吒割肉还父、剔骨还母，以身殉义。太乙真人以荷花、莲藕为材，将其魂魄重塑莲花法身，复活后的哪吒脚踏风火轮，手持火尖枪与乾坤圈，成为中国民间信仰中最受喜爱的少年神之一，勇猛而悲悯，永葆赤子之心。

stories.title_en: The Yellow Emperor Defeats Chi You
stories.summary_en: Chi You, the fearsome war deity with seventy-two brothers, all with bronze heads and iron brows who ate sand and iron, led the southern tribes in a great war against the Yellow Emperor Huangdi. Chi You summoned fog so thick that Huangdi's armies wandered lost for days. Huangdi responded by inventing the South-Pointing Chariot, a mechanical compass-device, to guide his troops. He also summoned the drought goddess Nüba and the heavenly dragon Yinglong. After terrible battles, Chi You was finally captured and executed. The Yellow Emperor unified the Central Plains and is venerated as the common ancestor of the Han Chinese people — the father of civilization who invented writing, medicine, the calendar, and music.
stories.summary_zh: 蚩尤有兄弟七十二人，铜头铁额，以砂石铁块为食，凶悍无比，率南方部族与黄帝展开大战。蚩尤呼风唤雾，大雾弥漫，黄帝军队迷失方向。黄帝发明指南车，引导军队辨别方向，又请来旱神女魃和天龙应龙助战。历经苦战，蚩尤终被擒杀。黄帝由此统一中原，被奉为华夏民族的共同祖先，相传文字、医学、历法、音乐皆源于其手。

stories.title_en: The Eight Immortals Cross the Sea
stories.summary_en: The Eight Immortals — Lü Dongbin, He Xiangu, Lan Caihe, Cao Guojiu, Han Xiangzi, Li Tieguai, Han Zhongli, and Zhang Guolao — were each humans who achieved immortality through extraordinary virtue or discipline. One day they were invited to the Jade Pool for the Queen Mother's Peach Banquet. Rather than take the easy celestial road, they each cast their personal treasure onto the sea surface and used it as a vessel: Li Tieguai's iron crutch, Lan Caihe's flower basket, He Xiangu's lotus, Han Zhongli's fan. The Dragon King's sons, jealous and violent, ambushed them and stole Lan Caihe. A cosmic battle erupted. The saying 'the Eight Immortals cross the sea, each showing their divine power' became a Chinese proverb for individual brilliance within collective effort.
stories.summary_zh: 八仙——吕洞宾、何仙姑、蓝采和、曹国舅、韩湘子、铁拐李、汉钟离、张果老——皆为凡人修炼成仙。一日赴瑶池参加蟠桃盛会，八仙不走天路，各以法宝投于海面为舟：铁拐李以铁拐，蓝采和以花篮，何仙姑以荷花，汉钟离以芭蕉扇。龙王之子妒忌生事，劫走蓝采和，引发一场轰天大战。"八仙过海，各显神通"由此成为家喻户晓的成语，比喻各人以独特才能共同完成大业。

stories.title_en: Liang Shanbo and Zhu Yingtai — The Butterfly Lovers
stories.summary_en: Zhu Yingtai, a girl forbidden from studying, disguised herself as a man to attend the academy. For three years she and the earnest scholar Liang Shanbo studied side by side, and she fell deeply in love with him, dropping countless hints he was too innocent to catch. When she finally had to return home, she revealed her sex and her feelings, but by then her father had already arranged her marriage to a wealthy man. Liang Shanbo died of heartbreak. On her wedding day, Zhu Yingtai insisted the procession pass by his tomb. As she wept at the graveside, the earth opened, and she leaped inside to join him. Two butterflies emerged from the tomb and flew together forever. Their story is called 'China's Romeo and Juliet' and is one of the four great Chinese folk legends.
stories.summary_zh: 祝英台女扮男装赴杭求学，与梁山伯同窗三载，情深意笃。她暗许芳心，多次含蓄示意，梁山伯却浑然不觉。临别之时，她坦白女儿身与深情，然而父亲早已将她许配富家。梁山伯相思成疾，含恨而终。出嫁之日，英台坚持让花轿绕行梁山伯坟前，她俯身痛哭，墓地骤然裂开，英台纵身跃入。旋即，一对彩蝶翩然而出，双飞不离。此传说被誉为"中国的罗密欧与朱丽叶"，列中国四大民间故事之首。

stories.title_en: Mulan Joins the Army
stories.summary_en: When the emperor issued a conscription order and every family was required to send one man to war, Hua Mulan's aged father was called but was too frail to survive the campaign. Having no elder brother, Mulan made a decision in the dark of night: she bought a horse and armor, bound her chest, and rode off to war in her father's place. For twelve years she fought in the northern campaigns, earning honor and rank, while never once revealing she was a woman. When the war ended, the emperor summoned her to court to award her a great position. She declined, asking only to return home. When her fellow soldiers came to visit, they were stunned to find their great commander in a dress, combing her hair at the mirror. 'How could we not have known?' they marveled. Mulan's story is a symbol of filial piety and female courage.
stories.summary_zh: 天子征兵，花木兰之父年迈体衰，家中又无兄长可代，木兰夜不能寐，毅然决定代父从军。她买马置甲，女扮男装，踏上北伐征途，驰骋沙场十二年，屡立战功，官至高位，始终未露女儿身份。战争结束，天子召见欲加封赏，木兰辞官不受，只求还乡侍奉双亲。昔日袍泽登门探望，见她对镜贴花黄，一袭女儿妆，无不大惊失色。木兰的故事千百年来传诵不衰，成为忠孝与巾帼英雄的永恒象征。

stories.title_en: Erlang Shen and the Heavenly Dog
stories.summary_en: Erlang Shen, also called Yang Jian, is the most powerful warrior deity in the Chinese pantheon — the nephew of the Jade Emperor who once split Mount Hua to rescue his mother. He has a third eye in the middle of his forehead that sees through all illusions and disguises, wields an iron staff of divine craftsmanship, and is accompanied by his loyal Heavenly Dog, whose teeth can bite through immortals. Erlang Shen was one of the few celestial warriors capable of matching the Monkey King in combat during the great battle of heaven. He is worshipped in many parts of China as the god of water control, as he is said to have tamed rivers and brought flood relief to Sichuan, completing the work begun by the legendary engineer Li Bing.
stories.summary_zh: 二郎神杨戬，玉皇大帝之甥，曾劈山救母，乃天庭第一战将。额间生有天眼，能识破一切幻术与伪装；手持三尖两刃刀，身旁跟随哮天犬，犬牙可伤神仙之体。大闹天宫之战中，二郎神是为数不多能与孙悟空势均力敌之神将。民间亦奉其为水神，相传他曾继续李冰治水之业，疏导川中江河，造福蜀地百姓。

stories.title_en: The Kitchen God Reports to Heaven
stories.summary_en: Zao Jun, the Kitchen God, lives behind the stove of every Chinese household and watches the family's behavior throughout the year. On the twenty-third day of the twelfth lunar month, he ascends to heaven to report to the Jade Emperor on the family's conduct — their quarrels, kindnesses, wastefulness, or virtues. Families burn a paper effigy of Zao Jun and smear his paper lips with honey or sticky candy so that he will only speak sweet words to the celestial court. A new paper image is then installed on New Year's Eve when he returns. The Kitchen God tradition reflects a beautifully practical theology: morality is observed not by distant gods but by the spirit who shares your hearth and knows every meal, every argument, every secret kindness.
stories.summary_zh: 灶王爷居于每户人家的灶台之后，全年守望一家老小的举止行为。腊月二十三，他升天向玉皇大帝禀报家中善恶。家人焚烧灶王纸像，并以糖果或糯米糖涂抹其嘴唇，祈望他只说甜言蜜语，在天庭多美言几句。除夕之夜，新的灶王像贴上，迎接他归家。灶王爷的信仰体现出一种朴实无华的民间神学：道德的监督不靠遥不可及的神明，而是那位与你共享一方烟火、知悉每一餐饭、每一次争吵与每一份善意的家灶守护神。

stories.title_en: The Jade Emperor and the Zodiac Race
stories.summary_en: The Jade Emperor declared that twelve animals would be chosen to mark the years of the calendar, their order determined by who arrived first at his celestial palace. The Rat woke early and hitched a ride on the Ox's back, then leaped ahead at the last moment to arrive first. The Ox came second; the Tiger, swimming strongly, third; the Rabbit, hopping across stones, fourth; the Dragon, who paused to bring rain to a drought-stricken village, fifth; the Snake, coiled around the Horse's hoof, sixth; Horse and Goat and Monkey and Rooster crossed together harmoniously; the Dog, distracted by a bath in the river, came eleventh; and the Pig, who stopped to eat and then nap, arrived last. The Cat, cheated out of a place by the Rat, was eternally excluded — which is why cats chase rats to this day.
stories.summary_zh: 玉皇大帝下令选十二种动物依次纪年，以抵达天宫的先后顺序为准。老鼠早起，骑在牛背上偷渡，临近终点一跃而下，抢得第一。牛排第二，虎游水抵达第三，兔踩石而渡得第四，龙中途降雨救旱获第五，蛇藏马蹄得第六，马、羊、猴、鸡依序相继，狗因贪玩洗澡排第十一，猪则睡懒觉殿后。猫被老鼠算计未能入选，因此猫鼠之间结下千古深仇，永无休止地追逐彼此。

stories.title_en: Guanyin the Goddess of Mercy
stories.summary_en: Guanyin — Guanshiyin, 'she who perceives the sounds of the world' — is the bodhisattva of compassion in Chinese Buddhism, a figure so beloved that she eclipses even the Buddha himself in popular devotion. She was once a princess named Miaoshan who refused an arranged marriage to pursue a spiritual life, and her father ordered her executed. But she was so pure of heart that the executioner's blade shattered and the ropes would not hold her. She descended into the underworld and transformed it into a paradise. Having achieved enlightenment, she chose not to enter Nirvana but to remain at the edge of the mortal world, listening to every cry of suffering and descending to help. She is depicted holding a willow branch dripping holy water and a white jade vase — she can hear you wherever you are.
stories.summary_zh: 观世音菩萨，意为「观察世间音声」，是中国佛教中慈悲的象征，其在民间的地位甚至超越佛陀本身。相传她前世为妙善公主，拒婚出家，遭父王下令处死，然而行刑之刃触及其身即断，绳索亦无法缚其。她魂入地府，以慈悲之力将其化为净土。证得菩提后，她放弃进入涅槃，选择驻守人间边际，倾听一切苦难之声，随缘救苦救难。她手持净瓶与杨柳枝，无论身在何处的众生发出呼唤，她皆能听见，皆能感应。

stories.title_en: Zhong Kui the Ghost Catcher
stories.summary_en: Zhong Kui was a brilliant scholar of the Tang Dynasty who passed the imperial examinations at the top of his class, but the Emperor rejected him as chief scholar on account of his terrifyingly ugly face. In despair, Zhong Kui threw himself against the palace steps and died. The Emperor, stricken with guilt, ordered him buried with honor. In the underworld Zhong Kui proved his worth: having died unjustly, he was granted the power to rule over demons and was appointed the Supreme Ruler of Ghosts by the King of Hell. He returned as a divine ghost hunter, wearing a bat-embroidered robe and carrying an iron sword, devouring malicious spirits whole. His image is traditionally hung above doorways during the Dragon Boat Festival to ward off evil.
stories.summary_zh: 唐代学子钟馗才学盖世，高中状元，然因容貌奇丑，被皇帝当廷黜落。他悲愤交加，撞阶而亡。皇帝追悔莫及，以状元之礼厚葬。钟馗含冤入冥，阎王感其刚烈，封其为天下都城隍，赐以降魔除鬼之权。自此，他身着蝙蝠纹袍，手持宝剑，横行鬼界，降妖伏魔，遇鬼即吞。民间端午节前后，常在门楣悬挂钟馗像以辟邪驱鬼。

stories.title_en: The Dragon Boat Festival and Qu Yuan
stories.summary_en: Qu Yuan was a poet-statesman of the state of Chu during the Warring States period, a man of deep patriotism who served as a senior advisor to King Huai. When court rivals slandered him and he was exiled, he wandered the countryside writing some of the most beautiful poetry in Chinese literary history, including the masterpiece Li Sao. When he learned that his beloved capital Ying had been captured by Qin troops, his grief was bottomless. On the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, 278 BCE, he walked into the Miluo River clutching a large stone and drowned himself. The people who loved him raced out in boats and beat drums to frighten away fish from devouring his body, threw rice dumplings into the water to feed the fish instead. These acts became the Dragon Boat races and zongzi dumplings we celebrate today.
stories.summary_zh: 屈原是战国时期楚国的爱国诗人与重臣，深受怀王器重。因小人谗言遭放逐，他漫游江湖，以笔墨寄托家国之思，写就《离骚》等流传千古的华章。公元前278年五月初五，得知郢都被秦军攻破，屈原悲痛欲绝，抱石投汨罗江，以身殉国。百姓闻讯，竞相驾舟打捞，以鼓声驱散鱼群，又投入粽子喂鱼，以免其蚕食屈原遗体。赛龙舟与吃粽子的习俗由此而生，沿袭至今。

stories.title_en: Mazu the Sea Goddess
stories.summary_en: Lin Moniang was born in 960 CE on Meizhou Island in Fujian. She was a prodigious child who spoke late but never cried, and was reportedly able to walk on water and fly across the sea. As a young woman she spent her days helping sailors navigate, predicting storms, and healing the sick. One night she fell into a trance while weaving — she was rescuing her father and brothers whose ships were caught in a storm. Her mother woke her before she could save the last brother, who drowned. She died young, at twenty-eight, and ascended directly to heaven. Sailors throughout the South China Sea began praying to her as a goddess, and she appeared to them in red robes during typhoons, calming the waters. Today Mazu is the most widely worshipped goddess in coastal China, Taiwan, and among Chinese diaspora worldwide.
stories.summary_zh: 林默娘生于宋太祖建隆元年，福建莆田湄洲岛人。她生而不哭，幼有神通，传说能行走于海面，飞渡大洋。她长年帮助渔民辨别海况、预测风暴、救死扶伤。一日夜间，她在家中织布时突然入定，正是魂游大海，搭救遭遇风暴的父兄。母亲将她唤醒，她已救出父亲与兄长，却未及搭救最小的弟弟，其弟因此溺亡。她二十八岁仙逝，魂升天界。南海渔民纷纷奉之为海神，传说台风之中有身着红袍的女神显灵护船。妈祖信仰至今仍是中国沿海、台湾及海外华人社区最广泛的民间信仰。

stories.title_en: The Fox Spirit Pu Songling
stories.summary_en: In the world of Pu Songling's 17th-century collection Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio, fox spirits are the most bewitching and morally complex beings imaginable. They are thousand-year-old foxes who have cultivated human form, usually appearing as beautiful women who fall in love with scholars. Unlike Western shape-shifters, Chinese fox spirits are neither simply evil nor simply good — they are drawn to human warmth, capable of genuine love, sometimes generous beyond measure. A fox wife might manage a poor scholar's household for years, bringing prosperity, only to vanish at the first sign of distrust. Their vulnerability is incense smoke and dog-barking. Their stories are allegories for love's transformation: the 'other' who mirrors our deepest desires and our capacity for cruelty.
stories.summary_zh: 蒲松龄的《聊斋志异》构建了一个狐仙横行的奇异世界。狐仙是修炼千年、化为人形的灵狐，多以绝色女子示人，倾情于书生文士。与西方怪物截然不同，中国狐仙不属善恶分明之辈——她们被人间烟火吸引，能够真情相爱，有时慷慨至极，一个狐仙妻子或许默默持家数年，为寒门书生带来富贵，却在遭受猜疑的那一刻悄然离去。香烟与狗吠是她们的天敌。她们的故事是爱与变幻的寓言：那个"他者"的镜像，映照出人类内心最深处的渴望与可能的残忍。

stories.title_en: The Monkey Who Stole the Peaches of Immortality
stories.summary_en: The Jade Emperor, to honor the Monkey King's self-proclaimed title of Great Sage Equal to Heaven, appointed him as guardian of the Heavenly Peach Garden — unaware that this was precisely the position that would cause the greatest disaster. The peach trees bloomed only once every three thousand to nine thousand years, and their fruit granted immortality to those who ate them. Sun Wukong, sampling one peach and then another, ate through the entire celestial orchard. When he discovered he had not been invited to the Queen Mother's Peach Banquet, he crashed the feast, ate all the immortality pills of Laozi, drank the Jade Emperor's celestial wine, and in a drunken stupor lay down to sleep. When he woke and realized what he had done, he fled back to his mountain. The Jade Emperor's fury assembled the entire celestial army against him.
stories.summary_zh: 玉皇大帝为安抚孙悟空的「齐天大圣」之名，封其掌管蟠桃园，却不知此举正是引祸之源。蟠桃三千年、六千年、九千年方熟一次，食之可得长生不老。孙悟空进园巡视，先尝一个，再尝一个，竟将满园仙桃悉数吃光。得知未受蟠桃宴之邀，他怒闯宴席，盗吃太上老君的仙丹，喝尽玉帝的御酒，大醉后倒头便睡。酒醒后察觉闯祸，急忙逃回花果山。玉帝震怒，集天兵天将，举兵征讨。

stories.title_en: The Legend of Nian the New Year Monster
stories.summary_en: Deep in the mountains lived a terrible beast called Nian — which means both 'year' and the creature's name. Every New Year's Eve it descended into villages to prey on livestock and children. For generations people fled to the mountains to hide. One year an old beggar appeared and was taken in by a kind old woman. When everyone else had gone, he stayed. That night when Nian appeared, the beggar's door blazed with red lanterns, firecrackers exploded, and the old man emerged in red robes. Nian fled in terror — it was horribly afraid of the color red, fire, and loud noise. The old woman returned to find the beggar gone, and realized he must have been an immortal. From then on people decorated in red, lit firecrackers, and hung lanterns every New Year's Eve. The word for celebrating New Year — 'guo nian,' 'passing the Nian' — preserves this ancient fear.
stories.summary_zh: 深山中有一头怪兽名为「年」，每逢除夕便下山进村，吞噬牲畜与孩童。世世代代，村民闻风丧胆，纷纷入山躲避。一年，来了位老乞丐，被一善良老妇收留。众人撤离，他独自留下。是夜，年兽出没，红灯高悬，爆竹声震，老人一袭红袍昂然而出。年兽落荒而逃——原来它最惧怕红色、火光与巨响。老妇归来，乞丐已杳无踪迹，众人方知他原是神仙下凡。此后，每逢除夕，家家张灯结彩、燃放爆竹、遍贴红色，"过年"一词由此而来，至今保存着这段古老的恐惧与守护。

stories.title_en: Amaterasu Emerges from the Cave
stories.summary_en: Amaterasu, goddess of the sun, quarreled bitterly with her brother Susanoo, the storm god, after he desecrated her sacred rice fields and broke holes in the walls of her weaving hall, causing her weaving maidens to die of fright. In grief and rage, Amaterasu sealed herself inside Ama-no-Iwato, the Heavenly Rock Cave, plunging the world into total darkness. Crops withered. Evil spirits ran amok. Eight million gods gathered outside the cave in desperation. The goddess Ame-no-Uzume began an ecstatic, bawdy dance, and the assembled gods roared with laughter so loud it shook the heavens. Amaterasu, burning with curiosity, inched the boulder aside to peer out — and was seized and pulled into the light. The world was bright again. This myth is the central origin myth of the Japanese imperial dynasty.
stories.summary_zh: 天照大神与弟弟素盏鸣尊（风暴之神）争执激烈，素盏鸣尊破坏神圣稻田、凿毁织布房，致使侍女惊吓而死。天照悲恸交加，躲入天之岩户，将自身封闭其中，天地随之陷入一片黑暗。万物枯萎，恶灵横行。八百万众神聚于洞外，束手无策。天宇受売命（ame-no-uzume）翩然起舞，诙谐狂野，众神哄笑声震天动地。天照按捺不住好奇，微启岩石向外窥探——众神趁机将她拉出洞外。天地重现光明。此神话是日本皇室最重要的起源传说。

stories.title_en: Momotaro the Peach Boy
stories.summary_en: An old childless couple lived alone, the woman washing clothes by the river. A giant peach came floating downstream, and when they cut it open, a healthy baby boy emerged. They named him Momotaro — Peach Boy — and raised him with love and wonder. He grew into a strong and righteous young man. When oni demons on Onigashima Island began terrorizing the region, Momotaro resolved to defeat them. He set out along the road, and one by one a dog, a monkey, and a pheasant joined him, each offered a portion of his divine millet dumpling. Together they crossed the sea to Onigashima, breached the demon fortress, defeated the Oni King in single combat, recovered stolen treasure, and returned it to the people. Momotaro is the archetypal Japanese hero — pure-hearted, egalitarian, courageous, and born from nature itself.
stories.summary_zh: 一对无子老夫妇相依为命，老婆婆在河边洗衣，见一只巨大的桃子顺流而来。剖开后，一个健壮的婴儿从中探出。老夫妇取名「桃太郎」，视若珍宝，精心养育。桃太郎长大成人，英武非凡。鬼岛上的鬼怪屡屡骚扰百姓，他立志征讨。一路行来，先后遇到狗、猴子与雉鸡，他以黍米糍粑相赠，三者相随出征。众人乘船渡海，攻克鬼城，桃太郎单枪匹马击败鬼王，夺回被掠财宝，分赠于民。桃太郎是日本民间故事的标志性英雄：心地纯良、为民除害、勇往直前，天生自然之子。

stories.title_en: Tanabata — The Star Festival
stories.title_zh: 七夕·织女与牛郎（日本版）
stories.summary_en: In the Japanese version of the celestial love story, Orihime (the weaver star Vega) is the daughter of the Sky King, who wove beautiful cloth day and night beside the Amanogawa — the Milky Way river. Lonely, she fell in love with Hikoboshi (the cowherd star Altair) who lived on the other side. Their love was so total that she stopped weaving and he neglected his cattle. The Sky King, enraged, separated them across the river. Orihime wept so bitterly that he allowed them to meet once a year, on the seventh night of the seventh month, when a flock of magpies forms a bridge. If it rains on that night, the bridge cannot form and they must wait another year. On Tanabata, people write wishes on colored strips of paper and hang them on bamboo, hoping their own dreams might cross whatever river separates them from what they love.
stories.summary_zh: 日本七夕传说中，织女星（织姬）是天帝之女，日夜于天河之畔织造美布。她爱上了河对岸的牧牛郎星（彦星），二人情深至此，她忘却织布，他疏于放牧。天帝震怒，以天河将二人永久分隔。织姬哭得肝肠寸断，天帝心软，许她每年七月七日与牛郎相会一次，鹊群搭桥为渡。若当夜降雨，桥无法形成，便只能再待一年。七夕之日，人们将心愿写在彩色短册上，系于竹枝，寄望自己的梦想也能跨越那道无形的天河。

stories.title_en: Urashima Taro and the Dragon Palace
stories.summary_en: A young fisherman named Urashima Taro rescued a sea turtle being tormented by children on the beach. The turtle, grateful, transformed into a beautiful princess and invited him to the Dragon Palace under the sea — Ryūgū-jō. There he spent what felt like three blissful days feasting and dancing, entertained by the Dragon King himself. When he wished to return to his village and his elderly mother, the princess gave him a beautiful lacquered box, Tamate-bako, and warned him never to open it. On the shore, he found everything changed: his house was gone, his mother was dead, and no one knew his name. Desperate, he opened the box. White smoke poured out, and in an instant he aged three hundred years and crumbled to dust. Time in the Dragon Palace had moved differently. The box held his age.
stories.summary_zh: 年轻渔夫浦岛太郎在海边救下一只遭孩子欺负的海龟。海龟化为美丽的公主，邀他前往海底龙宫。在那里，他以为仅仅度过了三天，实则享受了漫长的欢宴与舞乐，龙王亲自接待。他思念家乡与年迈母亲，公主依依不舍，赠他一只精美的玉匣"玉手箱"，叮嘱他切勿开启。回到岸上，一切物是人非：房屋消失，母亲已逝，无人识得他的名字。绝望之中，他开启玉匣，白烟袅袅而出，刹那间他苍老了三百岁，化为尘土消散。龙宫的时间与人间截然不同，匣中封存的，正是他失去的年华。

stories.title_en: Izanagi and Izanami Create the Islands
stories.title_zh: 伊邪那岐与伊邪那美创造日本列岛
stories.summary_en: In the beginning of Shinto creation, the divine couple Izanagi and Izanami stood on the Floating Bridge of Heaven and stirred the primordial ocean with a jeweled spear. When they lifted it, the brine dripping from its tip formed the first island, Onogoro. They descended and circled a pillar, met, and gave birth to the islands of Japan and the gods of wind, sea, mountains, trees, and rivers. But Izanami died giving birth to the fire god Kagutsuchi, whose heat burned her from within. Grief-stricken Izanagi descended into Yomi, the underworld, to bring her back. She warned him not to look at her. He lit his comb to see in the dark — and saw her body writhing with maggots and rotting demons. He fled in horror, rolled a boulder across the entrance to Yomi, and their marriage ended. She swore to kill a thousand people each day. He swore to create fifteen hundred births each day. Death and life are thus in perpetual balance.
stories.summary_zh: 神道创世之初，神圣夫妻伊邪那岐与伊邪那美站于天浮桥上，以天沼矛搅动混沌汪洋，矛尖滴落的海水凝成第一座岛屿——淤能碁吕岛。二神下凡，绕柱相遇，由此生下日本列岛及风、海、山、林诸神。然而伊邪那美因诞下火神迦具土，被灼烧而死。伊邪那岐悲痛万分，潜入黄泉寻妻，妻子叮嘱他切勿窥视。他以发梳点火，看见妻子身上爬满蛆虫、污秽腐烂，惊恐而逃，以巨石封堵黄泉入口。妻子誓言每日杀千人，他誓言每日生一千五百人。死与生由此永恒对立，循环不息。

stories.title_en: Kaguya-hime — The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter
stories.summary_en: A bamboo cutter discovered a tiny luminous girl inside a glowing bamboo stalk. He and his wife raised her as their daughter; she grew into a woman of impossible beauty, and they named her Kaguya-hime, the Shining Princess. Suitors arrived from across the land, including five noblemen each set impossible tasks and the Emperor himself. She turned them all away. Then she began weeping at every full moon, staring into the sky with inconsolable longing. Finally she revealed the truth: she was from the Moon Kingdom, banished to earth as punishment for some celestial offense, and the time of her return had come. On the night of the full moon, a celestial embassy descended on a cloud of light. They dressed her in the robe of feathers that erases earthly memory. She wept as she ascended, and sent the Emperor a letter and an elixir of immortality — which he, heartbroken, burned atop Mount Fuji.
stories.summary_zh: 竹取翁在竹林中发现一根闪闪发光的竹茎，内有拇指般大小的小女孩，收为养女。她迅速长大，美貌绝伦，名为「辉夜姬」。天下英雄慕名而来，五位权贵各被她以不可能完成之任务婉拒，就连天皇也未能例外。此后，她每逢月圆便仰天垂泪，思念之情难以言说。终于，她道出真相：她本是月宫之人，因罪被贬人间，如今归期已至。月圆之夜，天使乘光云降临，为她披上令人忘却尘世的羽衣，她含泪升天，留下一封信与一瓶长生不老药给天皇。天皇心碎，命人在富士山顶将药焚烧，据说那缕青烟，至今仍未消散。

stories.title_en: Yuki-onna the Snow Woman
stories.summary_en: On a night of terrible blizzard in the mountains of Japan, two woodcutters took shelter in a ferryman's hut. In the deep of night, a pale woman drifted through the walls — Yuki-onna, the Snow Woman, whose breath was the blizzard itself. She breathed on the elder woodcutter and he froze to death. She gazed at the younger, Mosaku, so beautiful that she spared him, warning him never to speak of what he had seen. Years later, a beautiful pale woman named Oyuki appeared at Mosaku's door on a winter night, saying she was lost. They fell in love and married, had children, and lived happily for years. One winter night, Mosaku told his wife about the strange woman in the snow — the secret he had kept so long. Oyuki's expression hardened. 'I am that woman,' she said. And she vanished, leaving only cold air and the sound of wind.
stories.summary_zh: 大雪漫天的山中夜晚，两名伐木工躲入渡船小屋避风雪。深夜，一个苍白的女子穿墙而入——这是雪女，她的呼吸本身便是暴风雪。她对着年老的伐木工吹气，老人随即冻死。她望向年轻的木佐（Mosaku），因他太过俊美，心生怜悯，放他一条生路，警告他绝口不提此事。数年后，一个名叫雪的苍白美女在冬夜叩响木佐的房门，自称迷了路。二人相爱成婚，生儿育女，岁月安稳。某个冬夜，木佐忍不住向妻子讲起那个雪中的怪异女子——那个他守口如瓶多年的秘密。雪的神情骤然冰冷："那便是我。"她就此消失，只留下一室寒气与风声。

stories.title_en: The Tengu of the Mountains
stories.summary_en: Tengu are the great mountain spirits of Japanese mythology — powerful, enigmatic, neither fully good nor evil. In their earliest form they were terrifying crow-demons, great black wings and beaks, harbingers of war. In later tradition they evolved into long-nosed, red-faced mountain sages in the robes of mountain ascetics, masters of swordsmanship and martial arts who test warriors seeking to become heroes. The most famous is Sojobo, the Great Tengu of Kurama Mountain, who took the young Minamoto no Yoshitsune as his student and trained him for years in the secret arts of war — knowledge the boy would use to help unify Japan. Tengu are still regarded with superstitious respect in Japan; their masks hang in mountain shrines as warnings against arrogance and pride.
stories.summary_zh: 天狗是日本神话中的山岳灵魂——威力无边，深不可测，不属善恶之列。最初的天狗形如乌鸦恶魔，黑翅巨喙，是战乱的凶兆。后世传说中，天狗演变为鼻长脸赤的山中仙人，身着修验道者服饰，精通剑术武艺，专门考验有志成为英雄的武士。其中最著名的是鞍马山大天狗僧正坊，相传曾收年幼的源义经为徒，数年间秘密传授其战阵之术——这段教导，最终助源义经完成了统一日本的壮举。至今日本山中神社中仍悬挂天狗面具，以警世人戒骄戒躁。

stories.title_en: Susanoo and the Eight-Headed Dragon
stories.summary_en: Exiled from heaven after his quarrel with Amaterasu, the storm god Susanoo descended to the land of Izumo, where he found an elderly couple weeping beside a river. They had once had eight daughters; every year, the monstrous eight-headed serpent Yamata no Orochi had come and devoured one. Only their last daughter, Kushinadahime, remained. Susanoo promised to slay the beast in exchange for her hand. He had the couple brew eight tubs of the strongest sake and set them out. When Orochi arrived — vast, its belly red from the fires it had passed through, moss growing on its back — it found the sake and each head drank from a different tub. As it fell into stupefied sleep, Susanoo drew his sword and hacked it to pieces. Inside one of the tails he found a magnificent sword: the Kusanagi, which became one of Japan's three imperial treasures.
stories.summary_zh: 素戔鸣尊被逐出天庭，流落至出云之国，见一对老夫妇在河边哭泣。他们本有八个女儿，每年都有一个被八岐大蛇吞噬，如今只剩最小的女儿奇稻田姬。素戔鸣尊允诺斩蛇，以奇稻田姬为妻。他命老夫妇酿造八大桶烈酒，依次置于沿岸。八岐大蛇来袭——身躯庞大，腹部被所经之火烤得通红，背上苔藓丛生——八个头各自饮尽一桶烈酒，沉沉酣睡。素戔鸣尊拔刀将其斩为碎块，在其中一条尾巴中发现了一把神剑——草薙之剑，由此成为日本皇室三大神器之一。

stories.title_en: The Legend of Dangun — Founding of Korea
stories.summary_en: Hwanung, the son of the Lord of Heaven, desired to live among humans and descended to Mount Taebaek with three thousand followers, bringing with him the Wind Earl, the Rain Master, and the Cloud Master. He governed with 360 areas of civilization: agriculture, medicine, justice, and the arts. A bear and a tiger both wished to become human and prayed to Hwanung. He gave them a bundle of sacred mugwort and twenty cloves of garlic, instructing them to eat only these and remain in their cave for one hundred days, away from sunlight. The tiger lost patience and fled. The bear endured, and on the twenty-first day transformed into a beautiful woman. She married Hwanung and gave birth to a son: Dangun Wanggeom, who founded the nation of Gojoseon in 2333 BCE — the first Korean kingdom. Koreans traditionally count their civilization from this date.
stories.summary_zh: 天帝之子桓雄渴望居于人间，携三千部众降临太白山，带来风伯、雨师、云师，治理人间三百六十事，涵盖农业、医术、律法与艺术。一只熊与一只虎共同祈愿，求化为人形，桓雄赐予它们艾蒿与二十瓣大蒜，命其在山洞中百日不见阳光，唯以此为食。虎性急，中途出走；熊坚忍不拔，第二十一日化为美丽女子。女子与桓雄成婚，诞下一子——檀君王俭，于公元前2333年建立古朝鲜，开创朝鲜半岛第一个王国。朝鲜民族的文明史，由此算起。

stories.title_en: Jumong the Divine Archer — Founding of Goguryeo
stories.title_zh: 朱蒙建国·高句丽开国神话
stories.summary_en: Haemosu, the son of the Heavenly Emperor, met and seduced Yuhwa, a daughter of the River God, on the banks of the Yalu River. Her father, furious at the dishonor, had her imprisoned in a sunlit room. There she became pregnant with the sunlight itself, and gave birth to an egg. The local king, frightened by the egg's strangeness, tried to feed it to horses and pigs — but they all refused to eat it. He left it in the wilderness, but birds came and sheltered it with their wings. The egg hatched and revealed a baby boy of supernatural abilities who grew into the greatest archer in the kingdom. Resentful nobles tried to kill him. He fled south, where rivers magically parted for him. He founded the kingdom of Goguryeo in 37 BCE and named himself Jumong — Skilled Archer — a title that became legend.
stories.summary_zh: 天帝之子解慕漱在鸭绿江畔邂逅河伯之女柳花，私定情缘。河伯大怒，将女儿囚于日光之室。柳花受阳光感孕，产下一枚大卵。当地君王惧其奇异，令马匹与猪群踩踏吞食，皆不为所动。卵被抛于荒野，鸟兽纷纷聚拢护翼。卵终于孵化，降生一名天赋异禀的男孩，长大后成为举国无双的神射手。权贵嫉妒欲加害，他南逃出走，行至江河，水流竟自动分开为他让路。公元前37年，他建立高句丽，自号"朱蒙"，意为"善射者"，由此流传千古。

stories.title_en: The Story of Chunhyang
stories.summary_en: In the city of Namwon during the Joseon Dynasty, a nobleman's son named Yi Mongryong fell in love with Chunhyang, the daughter of a retired courtesan. Though of different social classes, they pledged eternal fidelity and secretly married. Yi was summoned to Seoul for the state examinations, leaving Chunhyang behind. In his absence, a corrupt new governor demanded Chunhyang become his concubine. She refused with absolute dignity, was beaten and imprisoned. Yi passed the examinations at the top of his class and was appointed as a royal inspector. He traveled to Namwon in disguise, assessed the governor's corruption, and on the day of Chunhyang's scheduled execution, revealed himself and arrested the governor. The story of Chunhyang is one of the most beloved works of Korean literature — a love story, a social critique, and a portrait of a woman whose integrity no power could break.
stories.summary_zh: 朝鲜王朝时期，南原城贵族子弟李梦龙爱上了妓生之女春香，二人誓约同心，秘密成婚。不久，李梦龙被召赴京赶考，含泪与春香离别。其间，新任贪官变使道强迫春香为妾，她以铮铮铁骨断然拒绝，遭受毒打，身陷囹圄。李梦龙高中状元，奉命出任暗行御史，乔装来到南原，查清贪官罪行。在春香即将被处决的那日，他揭开身份，当场将恶官逮捕归案。《春香传》是朝鲜古典文学中最脍炙人口的作品，集爱情故事、社会批判与女性气节于一体，传唱数百年不衰。

stories.title_en: Bari Gongju the Abandoned Princess
stories.summary_en: A king and queen desperately wanted a son, but their seventh child was again a daughter. In despair the king abandoned the baby girl in a box in the sea. She was found and raised by an old couple at the edge of the underworld. Years later, the king fell terribly ill and was told only medicine from the Western Underworld could save him. None of his six beloved daughters would undertake the terrible journey. Word reached the abandoned seventh daughter, Bari. She said: even a father who abandoned me is still my father. She traveled through three thousand ri of thorns and fire to the underworld, spent nine years there in labor to pay for the medicine, and returned to heal her father just as he was dying. For her boundless compassion Bari was made the goddess of shamanism, the divine guide who shepherds the souls of the dead to the afterlife. Korean shamans invoke her in every death ritual.
stories.summary_zh: 国王与王后渴望子嗣，第七个孩子仍是女儿。国王绝望之下，将婴儿装入木箱，抛入大海。女孩被幽冥边界的老夫妇所救，得以成长。多年后，国王身染重病，传说唯有西方冥界之药方可救治。六个备受宠爱的公主无人愿赴此险途。消息传至被遗弃的第七女巴里，她说：抛弃我的父亲，仍是我的父亲。她穿越三千里荆棘烈火，抵达冥界，以九年辛劳换取仙药，归来之际，父王已奄奄一息，药到病除。巴里因无量慈悲，被封为萨满教主神，引导亡魂前往来生的神圣使者。韩国萨满师在每一场丧葬仪式中，皆会召请巴里公主降临。

stories.title_en: Haenyeo — The Sea Women of Jeju
stories.summary_en: The haenyeo of Jeju Island — female divers who hold their breath and descend to extraordinary depths to harvest abalone, conch, and sea urchin — have their own mythological origin. The sea goddess Jamsugwishin, herself a diver who once drowned beneath the waves, is said to protect the haenyeo in exchange for offerings and respect. The women dive without equipment, using only their lung capacity and their knowledge of the sea bottom, the currents, and the creatures. They dive from childhood to old age, some working past eighty. They are a matriarchal community; for centuries their earnings supported Jeju families while their husbands stayed ashore. Their haunting whistle — the sumbisori, the sound they make releasing breath — is unique in the world. UNESCO listed haenyeo culture as an Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2016.
stories.summary_zh: 济州岛海女是屏息潜入深海采集鲍鱼、海螺与海胆的女性潜水者，其文化渊源流长，富有神话色彩。相传有一位潜水溺亡的海神"潜水鬼神"，在接受供奉与尊敬的条件下，守护着出海的海女们。她们不借助任何潜水设备，仅凭肺活量、对海底地形、洋流与海洋生物的深厚认知，从幼年潜至暮年，有人甚至年过八旬仍在耕海。海女社区以女性为中心，数百年来以她们的收入维持济州家庭的生计，丈夫们则留守岸边。她们吐气时发出的独特哨音——「苏木毕哨声」，是世界上独一无二的声响。2016年，联合国教科文组织将海女文化列入非物质文化遗产。

stories.title_en: The Gumiho — Nine-Tailed Fox of Korea
stories.summary_en: The Korean Gumiho is a fox that has lived for a thousand years and grown nine tails — a creature of great supernatural power, almost always depicted as female and dangerous. Unlike the Chinese fox spirit which can be benevolent, the Gumiho in Korean folklore is almost exclusively predatory: she appears as a beautiful woman, seduces men, and devours their livers or hearts to accumulate human energy. Yet some versions tell of a Gumiho who desperately wants to become human. If she can go one thousand days without eating human flesh and without being exposed, she will transform. But humans always discover her, always betray her, always drive her back. The Gumiho stands at the border between the human and the monstrous — a being whose longing for humanity makes her more human than the humans who reject her.
stories.summary_zh: 韩国的九尾狐是修炼千年、尾生九条的灵狐，拥有强大的妖力，几乎清一色被描述为女性，且充满危险。与中国狐仙或善或恶的多面形象不同，韩国传说中的九尾狐几乎专事捕猎——她化为绝色美人，诱惑男子，取食其肝脏或心脏，以积累人类气息。然而，部分版本中存在一种渴望化为人类的九尾狐：若她能坚持一千天不食人肉、不被发现，便能真正成为人类。然而人类总会察觉，总会背叛，总会将她驱逐。九尾狐游走于人与怪物的边界，她对人性的渴望，有时远比那些拒绝她的人更加真诚。

stories.title_en: Lac Long Quan and Au Co — The Origin of Vietnam
stories.title_zh: 貉龙君与甌姬·越南民族起源
stories.summary_en: In the mythological dawn of Vietnam, the Dragon Lord Lac Long Quan, son of the Dragon King of the Eastern Sea, met and married Au Co, a mountain fairy of celestial beauty descended from the immortals of the high peaks. Their union produced a miraculous pregnancy: Au Co gave birth to a sac containing one hundred eggs, from which hatched one hundred strong sons — the ancestors of the Vietnamese people. But a Dragon Lord and a Mountain Fairy cannot share the same world: he belonged to the sea and she to the mountains, and their separation was inevitable. He took fifty sons with him to the coast; she took fifty to the mountains. Their eldest son became the first Hung King, founder of the Van Lang kingdom — the ancestor state of Vietnam. Every Vietnamese person today traces symbolic descent from this union of sea and mountain.
stories.summary_zh: 在越南创世神话的黎明，东海龙王之子貉龙君与从仙山降世的神仙甌姬相遇，缔结姻缘，诞下一囊百卵，孵出百名健壮男儿——越南民族的先祖。然而龙族与仙界本属相异，海洋与山岳终需分离：他带走五十个儿子前往沿海，她带走五十个儿子归返山中。长子成为第一位雄王，建立文郎国——越南最早的祖先国家。时至今日，每位越南人都在象征意义上追溯自己的血脉，来自这场海与山的相遇。

stories.title_en: The Trung Sisters Rise Against Han Rule
stories.summary_en: In 40 CE, two sisters — Trung Trac and Trung Nhi — led one of history's most remarkable uprisings against Chinese Han Dynasty occupation of Vietnam. When Trung Trac's husband was executed by the cruel Han governor Su Ding for political resistance, she did not grieve in silence. She and her sister raised an army of eighty thousand, recruited thirty-six female generals, marched on sixty-five fortresses, and drove the Han forces from the country. Trung Trac declared herself queen, abolished the hated taxes, and ruled the newly liberated land. She governed for three years. When the Han emperor sent a great general with a massive army, the sisters were defeated. Rather than be captured, they threw themselves into the Hat Giang river. They are among Vietnam's most venerated national heroes, their statues standing in temples throughout the country, honored on the sixth day of the second lunar month.
stories.summary_zh: 公元40年，征侧与征贰两姐妹领导了历史上最壮烈的抗汉起义。征侧之夫因反抗暴政而遭汉朝太守苏定处决，征侧没有沉默哀伤，而是与妹妹高举义旗，召集八万义军，延揽三十六位女将，攻克六十五座城池，将汉军逐出越南国境。征侧自立为王，废除苛税，统治光复之土。她执政三年，后遭汉帝遣派大军反攻，节节败退。两姐妹宁死不降，纵身投入帽江。她们是越南最受崇敬的民族英雄之一，全国各地庙宇均立有她们的神像，每年阴历二月初六举行盛大祭典。

stories.title_en: The Magic Crossbow of An Duong Vuong
stories.summary_en: King An Duong Vuong ruled the ancient kingdom of Au Lac and built the spiral citadel of Co Loa, aided by a golden turtle god who gave him the claw of a divine turtle to make a magic crossbow that could kill one thousand enemies with a single shot. His kingdom seemed invincible. A neighboring Chinese lord, Trieu Da, could not defeat him militarily, so he sent his son Trong Thuy to marry the king's daughter My Chau. The young man, truly falling in love with My Chau but fulfilling his father's command, secretly switched the magic crossbow with a fake. When Trieu Da attacked, An Duong Vuong fled with his daughter, shooting arrows along the way. When he reached the sea and called on the turtle god, the god appeared and declared: 'The enemy rides behind you.' My Chau had been leaving a trail of goose feathers from her cloak to guide her husband. The king drew his sword and killed his daughter. He walked into the sea. Trong Thuy drowned himself in the well at Co Loa where My Chau had bathed.
stories.summary_zh: 安阳王统治古越南甌雒国，在金龟神赐予的神龟爪的帮助下建起螺旋形的螺城，以此为原料铸造的神弩可一箭射杀千敌，举国无忧。北方赵达无法以武力征服，便遣子仲始迎娶公主媚珠。仲始真情爱上媚珠，却遵父命暗中将神弩调换。赵达来袭，安阳王携女仓皇出逃，不住射箭断后。抵达海边，金龟神显现，告曰："敌人就在你身后。"媚珠一路将羽衣碎羽撒下，为丈夫引路。安阳王含泪拔剑，亲手杀死爱女，随即步入海中。仲始闻讯，投身媚珠曾沐浴的螺城井中自尽。

stories.title_en: Geser Khan the Cosmic Warrior
stories.summary_en: The Epic of King Geser is the longest epic poem in world literature — over one million lines in some versions — and the national epic of Tibet, Mongolia, and many Central Asian peoples. Geser is a divine warrior-king born into a humble family, whose celestial origins are gradually revealed as he battles demons and monsters threatening to destroy the human world. He rides a miraculous horse, wields weapons made by celestial smiths, and is guided by his divine mother from heaven. His battles are cosmic in scale: he defeats the lords of the four directions, descends to hell to rescue his mother, and defeats the greatest demon kings. He is still revered as a living deity in Tibet and Mongolia, his cult so powerful that his image adorns temples, and shamans claim to channel his spirit in ritual performance.
stories.summary_zh: 《格萨尔王传》是世界上最长的史诗，某些版本超过百万行，是西藏、蒙古及众多中亚民族的民族英雄史诗。格萨尔以平凡的身世降临人间，随着他征讨威胁人类的魔鬼与怪物，其天界出身逐渐显现。他胯下骑着神马，手持天界铸造的神兵，受天上母亲护持指引。他的征战规模宏大：击败四方魔王，下入地狱救出母亲，诛灭最强大的魔王。至今，他仍作为活神明受到西藏与蒙古的崇敬，寺庙中遍布其神像，萨满师宣称在仪式中能请其附身降临。

stories.title_en: Milarepa the Sinful Magician Who Became a Saint
stories.summary_en: Milarepa was born into prosperity in 11th-century Tibet, but his father died young and his relatives seized the family estate, reducing his mother and sister to servitude. His mother, burning with humiliation, sent Milarepa to study black magic. He returned and called down hailstorms that destroyed his relatives' harvest and collapsed a house during a wedding, killing thirty-five people. The horror of what he had done broke him open. He sought out the great teacher Marpa, who refused to teach him until Milarepa had suffered sufficiently — he was made to build, alone and by hand, a tower of stone and then tear it down three times. Finally Marpa accepted him. Milarepa meditated alone in caves for years, surviving on nettle soup until his skin turned green, and achieved complete enlightenment. He became one of Tibet's greatest poet-saints, whose spontaneous spiritual songs are still sung today.
stories.summary_zh: 密勒日巴生于11世纪西藏富贵之家，父亲早逝，亲戚夺占家产，将其母亲与妹妹沦为仆役。母亲含辱，命他外出习学黑魔法。他学成归来，施法降下冰雹，毁掉亲戚的庄稼，又在婚宴中使房屋坍塌，三十五人罹难。所犯之罪令他彻底崩溃，良知觉醒。他求见上师马尔巴，马尔巴拒绝传法，令他独自以双手建造石塔，再亲手推倒，反复三次。历经磨难，马尔巴终于收他为徒。密勒日巴此后独居洞窟数年，以荨麻汤为食，皮肤竟染成绿色，最终证得圆满佛果。他成为西藏最伟大的诗僧之一，所唱道歌至今仍被传诵。

stories.title_en: Gesar of Ling and the Snow Lion
stories.summary_en: Among the many episodes of the Gesar epic, one of the most beloved involves his bond with the Snow Lion — the mythical animal of Tibet, king of beasts, whose turquoise mane matches the sky and whose roar causes mountains to tremble. The Snow Lion represents the fearless joy and the unconquerable sovereignty of Tibet itself. It is one of four supernatural beings — with the Garuda, the Dragon, and the Tiger — that together symbolize the four directions and four qualities of enlightened power. In the Gesar cycle, Geser is associated with the Snow Lion as a sign of his divine kingship and his inseparable connection to the Tibetan land. Snow Lion imagery adorns the Tibetan national flag, thangka paintings, and temple pillars throughout the Himalayan world.
stories.summary_zh: 在格萨尔史诗众多篇章中，最为人传颂的之一，便是他与雪狮之间的神圣羁绊。雪狮是西藏神话中的百兽之王，鬃毛碧蓝如天，一声怒吼令山岳颤抖，象征着西藏本身所具有的无畏喜悦与不可征服的主权。雪狮与大鹏金翅鸟、龙、虎并列为西藏四大神兽，各司四方，各代表觉悟之力的一个面向。在格萨尔史诗体系中，格萨尔与雪狮的联结象征他的神圣王权，以及他与西藏大地之间不可分割的灵性纽带。雪狮图案至今装饰于西藏国旗、唐卡画作与喜马拉雅各地寺庙的廊柱之上。

stories.title_en: Genghis Khan and the Blue Wolf
stories.summary_en: The Secret History of the Mongols — the oldest surviving Mongolian literary work, written in the 13th century — begins with the founding myth of the Mongol people. Their ancestor was Börte Chino, the Blue-Grey Wolf, who crossed a vast inland sea and mated with Goo Maral, the Fallow Doe. From this union of sky-blue wolf and earth-pale deer came the lineage that would eventually produce Temüjin — who became Genghis Khan. The wolf represents heaven, masculine, directional power; the deer represents earth, feminine, gentleness. Mongolians believed themselves descended from this cosmic marriage. Throughout his campaigns, Genghis Khan honored the wolf as his ancestral spirit, and Mongolian shamanic tradition still reveres the Blue Wolf as a protective spirit of extraordinary power.
stories.summary_zh: 《蒙古秘史》是现存最古老的蒙古文学著作，成书于13世纪，开篇即是蒙古民族的创世神话：祖先为苍狼孛儿帖赤那，渡越浩瀚大湖，与白鹿豁埃马阑结合。这场苍狼与白鹿的结合，天地阴阳的交融，孕育出了一脉传承，最终诞生了铁木真——即成吉思汗。狼代表苍天、阳性与方向之力；鹿代表大地、阴柔与包容。蒙古人相信自己是这场宇宙婚配的后裔。成吉思汗一生南征北战，始终以狼为祖先神灵加以崇敬，蒙古萨满传统至今仍将苍狼奉为拥有强大护佑力量的守护灵。

stories.title_en: Erlik — Lord of the Underworld
stories.summary_en: In the shamanic cosmology of the Mongols and Turkic peoples, Erlik is the king of the underworld — the first being who sought more power than the creator god Ulgen had allotted him. He was cast down into the lower worlds beneath the roots of the cosmic tree, where he rules seventeen levels of the underworld and commands an army of demons. He is neither simply evil nor simply dark — he is necessary, a counterbalance. His domain is death, but also the vital, raw, animal forces of life. Shamans who seek the most powerful knowledge must descend through his realms, negotiate with him, and return — bringing back souls of the sick or secrets of the dead. He is depicted as an enormous black man with bull-horns and a beard to his knees, surrounded by his children who carry out his judgments across the earth.
stories.summary_zh: 在蒙古与突厥民族的萨满宇宙观中，额尔力克是冥界之王——最初他是创世神乌尔根（Ulgen）麾下的存在，却因觊觎超越神授的权力而被打入宇宙树根部以下的冥界深处，统辖十七层地下世界，麾下魔军无数。他并非纯粹的邪恶或黑暗之神——他是必要的存在，是宇宙的另一极。他的领域是死亡，也是生命最原始、最本能的动力。萨满师若求最深之知，必须穿越他的国度，与他谈判，再带着病人的灵魂或亡者的秘密返回人间。他被描绘为头生牛角、胡须及膝的巨大黑色男神，身边是代他在人间执行裁决的子女们。

stories.title_en: The Blessed Naadam Games — Origin Myth
stories.summary_en: Naadam — the Mongolian festival of the Three Games of Men: wrestling, archery, and horse racing — has a mythological origin woven into nomadic cosmology. The three games are not merely contests of skill but reenactments of the heroic virtues that allowed the Mongol people to survive and dominate the steppes. Wrestling echoes the battles of shamanic spirits; archery mirrors Tengri, the sky god, whose arrows are lightning; horse racing honors the sacred bond between Mongol and horse — an animal considered so spiritually significant that certain horses were never ridden but kept as living offerings to heaven. The festival takes place in July under the vast open sky, believed to be watched over by Tengri himself, who evaluates the strength of the people and the vitality of the nation.
stories.summary_zh: 那达慕——蒙古民族的男子三项传统竞技节：摔跤、射箭与赛马——其起源深植于游牧民族的宇宙观之中。三项比赛绝非单纯的技艺较量，而是重现蒙古人得以在草原存活与壮大的英雄美德。摔跤呼应萨满灵魂之战，射箭映射长生天腾格里的雷电之箭，赛马则彰显蒙古人与马的神圣羁绊——某些马匹被视为神圣，终生不骑，以活体供奉于天。那达慕在七月辽阔的苍天之下举行，据信腾格里神明在上俯瞰，审视民族的力量与国家的生命力。

stories.title_en: The Fox Marries the Scholar
stories.summary_en: A variation of the Chinese fox-spirit tradition unique to Shandong Province tells of a poor scholar who discovers a beautiful woman living secretly in his ancestral shrine. She is a fox spirit who has been protecting his family for three generations. She reveals herself honestly, offers to help him pass his examinations with her supernatural knowledge of texts, and they fall in love. They live as husband and wife for seven years, have two children. But the scholar's mother suspects something inhuman about her daughter-in-law and consults a Daoist priest, who drives the fox out with charms and burning incense. She leaves without anger, telling her children to honor their grandmother. The scholar fails every examination thereafter without her help. He spends the rest of his life writing down her stories, which are said to be the real source material for Pu Songling's Strange Stories.
stories.summary_zh: 流传于山东的一则狐仙故事别具一格：一位贫寒书生发现有美丽女子秘居于祖先祠堂之中。她是一只狐仙，已默默庇护他家三代。她坦然现出身份，以妖界对典籍的超凡理解助书生备考，两人情投意合，缔结连理，育有两子，共度七年。然而书生之母始终疑心儿媳有异，请来道士以符咒与燃香将其驱走。狐妻离去时毫无怨怼，嘱咐孩子们好好孝顺祖母。书生此后屡试不第，余生将她的故事一一记录成文，据说这便是蒲松龄《聊斋志异》真正的素材来源。

stories.title_en: The Ghost Festival and Hungry Ghosts
stories.summary_en: On the fifteenth day of the seventh lunar month, the gates of the underworld open and the spirits of the dead are free to roam the earth. Among them are the Hungry Ghosts — souls condemned to wander starving, their throats too narrow to swallow, their stomachs swollen with emptiness — because they lived lives of greed, selfishness, or died without descendants to honor them. In the Buddhist origin story, the monk Mulian used his supernatural powers to see his mother suffering in the realm of hungry ghosts. The Buddha told him that only the collective merit of the entire monastic community, offered together, could liberate her. This gave rise to the Ghost Festival's central rituals: burning paper money and offerings, laying out feasts for wandering spirits, performing rituals on the street to feed and pacify the restless dead.
stories.summary_zh: 农历七月十五，冥界之门大开，亡灵自由游走于人间。其中有一类"饿鬼"——因生前贪婪自私，或无后人祭祀而流离失所，被罚咽喉细窄无法进食、腹空如鼓、永久饥渴。佛教典故中，大目犍连以神通看见母亲身陷饿鬼道，苦不堪言。佛陀告知，唯有全体僧团共同以功德回向，方可超度其母出苦海。由此生出盂兰盆节的核心仪式：焚烧纸钱与供品，为游魂设宴，在街头举行法事，以安抚、供养那些无所归依的亡灵。

stories.title_en: The God of War Guan Yu
stories.summary_en: Guan Yu was a real historical general of the Three Kingdoms period who served the warlord Liu Bei with legendary loyalty. He was captured by the rival Cao Cao, who offered him riches, rank, and glory if he would switch allegiance. He declined every offer with courtesy and dignity and returned to Liu Bei's side at the first opportunity, escorting Cao Cao's wives across enemy territory safely as a final act of honor. He was eventually captured and executed by the kingdom of Wu in 219 CE. Within decades of his death, people were already building temples to him. The emperor of heaven is said to have appeared to a monk and declared that Guan Yu had been elevated to divine rank. By the Qing Dynasty he had become one of China's highest deities: the God of War, God of Brotherhood, patron of merchants, police, and martial arts practitioners alike.
stories.summary_zh: 关羽是三国时期真实存在的历史名将，以对刘备忠贞不二的气节名垂青史。被曹操俘获后，曹操以高官厚禄百般劝降，关羽以礼相拒，丝毫不动摇。一俟时机，他护送曹操妻眷安然过境，只身返回刘备阵营，全始全终。公元219年，他终被东吴所擒，慷慨就义。其身后数十年，民间已自发建庙奉祀。传说天帝托梦高僧，宣告关羽晋升神位。至清朝，他已成为中国地位最高的神明之一：武圣、义帝、商人与警察的守护神，武艺修习者共同供奉的祖师。

stories.title_en: The Princess Who Would Not Marry
stories.summary_en: In a Korean folk tale beloved across the peninsula, a princess who was brilliant, learned, and deeply independent refused every suitor her father presented. To every prince and nobleman she put impossible questions that none could answer. The King, frustrated, declared she must marry the next man who came to the palace gate. The next morning a poor farmer's boy arrived delivering rice, not knowing what awaited him. The princess put her impossible questions to him too. The boy — raised close to the earth, to planting seasons and river floods and animal births — answered every one with simple, direct wisdom. The princess, who had spent her life waiting for someone who saw the world as clearly as she did, accepted him. The story ends not with rescue but with recognition: the prince who wins the clever princess is not stronger or richer, just as curious.
stories.summary_zh: 这是一个流传于朝鲜半岛的民间故事。一位聪慧独立的公主拒绝了父王安排的所有求亲，对每位王子与贵族提出无人能答的刁钻问题。国王无奈，宣布必须嫁给次日第一个踏入宫门之人。次日清晨，一个不知内情的贫农少年送粮而至。公主再次以难题相问，少年自幼与土地为伍，熟悉节气、洪涝与生灵之道，以朴实无华的智慧一一作答。公主毕生等待一个像她一样清醒地看待世界的人，终于找到了。故事的结尾不是被拯救，而是被看见：赢得聪明公主的，不是最强或最富的人，而是同样充满好奇心的人。

stories.title_en: The Dragon Gate and the Jumping Carp
stories.summary_en: On the Yellow River, where the current runs fastest and the water crashes through a narrow gorge at Longmen — Dragon Gate — the ancient Chinese believed that any carp strong and determined enough to swim against the current and leap through the gate would be transformed into a dragon. Thousands of fish attempted the feat every spring; most failed and fell back into the river. But any who succeeded leaped from the world of common creatures into the world of the divine. The legend became the central metaphor for the imperial examination system: common-born scholars who studied for years and passed the brutal national exam were said to have 'leaped through Dragon Gate' — transformed from common men into officials who could shape the fate of the empire. The phrase 'the carp leaps through Dragon Gate' became a Chinese idiom for transformative success through effort.
stories.summary_zh: 黄河流经龙门峡谷，水流湍急，浪涛奔腾。古人相信，凡鲤鱼中有足够强悍坚毅者，能逆流跃过龙门，便可化身为龙。每年春天，成千上万的鱼儿竞相奋跃，大多数力竭落回河中，唯有成功者从凡鱼之境跃入神龙之界。这个传说后来成为科举制度最核心的隐喻：寒门学子苦读多年，通过严酷的国家大考，被称为"跃过龙门"——从平民摇身一变为能够左右帝国命运的官员。"鲤鱼跳龙门"由此成为中国最广为人知的成语，象征着以努力实现的脱胎换骨。

stories.title_en: The Monkey and the Tortoise — A Southeast Asian Fable
stories.title_zh: 猴子与乌龟（东南亚寓言）
stories.summary_en: A fable widespread across Vietnam, the Philippines, and surrounding regions tells of a clever monkey who convinced a tortoise to carry him across a river, promising to help him find food in return. Once safely across, the monkey climbed a banana tree and ate alone, throwing only the skins down to the tortoise waiting below. The tortoise said nothing. When they needed to cross back, the monkey again asked for a ride. This time the tortoise swam to the middle of the deepest part of the river and asked: 'Can you swim?' The monkey admitted he could not. 'Then you should have shared your bananas,' said the tortoise, and dove under. The monkey barely made it to shore, clutching driftwood. The moral is delivered without commentary: equity is not generosity — it is what makes survival together possible.
stories.summary_zh: 这则寓言在越南、菲律宾及周边地区广泛流传。一只聪明的猴子央求乌龟驮他渡河，承诺回报以食物。安全到岸后，猴子爬上香蕉树独自饱食，只把果皮扔给在下面等候的乌龟，乌龟一声不吭。需要返回时，猴子再次请求搭背渡河。这一次，乌龟游到河流最深处，问道："你会游泳吗？"猴子坦言不会。"那你就该分享你的香蕉，"乌龟说完，潜入水底。猴子拼命抓住浮木，勉强爬回岸边。这则故事不加任何说教：公平并非慷慨之举，而是共同生存的前提。

stories.title_en: The Iron-Crutch Li and His Wandering Soul
stories.summary_en: Li Tieguai — Iron-Crutch Li, one of the Eight Immortals — has the strangest origin story in the pantheon. He was a handsome, able-bodied man who studied Taoism under Laozi himself. One day he left his body in the care of his disciple while his soul flew to Laozi's heaven for further teaching, warning the disciple to keep watch for seven days. On the sixth day, the disciple's mother fell ill, and in distress he burned Li's body. When the soul returned, there was no body to enter. Li's soul, homeless, entered the first body available: a recently deceased beggar, emaciated and ugly, with one lame leg. He used a gourd and iron crutch to get around. He never tried to find a better body. Instead he accepted this grotesque vessel and continued his spiritual practice, eventually becoming one of the most powerful of the Immortals — proof that the body is merely a dwelling.
stories.summary_zh: 铁拐李是八仙中来历最为奇特的一位。他曾是体健貌端的修道者，直接拜太上老君为师。一日，他嘱托弟子守护自己的肉身七日，而灵魂飞赴天界向老君求教。第六日，弟子之母突然病危，弟子惊慌之中将师父的肉身烧化。灵魂归来，无处可栖，情急之下附入附近刚刚死去的乞丐枯骨——瘦骨嶙峋，貌丑无比，还有一条残腿。他以铁拐与葫芦为伴行走于世，从未寻觅更好的躯体。反而坦然接受这副丑陋皮囊，继续修炼，最终成为八仙中法力最强者之一——以亲身示范，证明肉身不过是一处暂居之所。

stories.title_en: The Hungry Tiger Who Became a Guardian
stories.title_zh: 饥饿的老虎成为守护神
stories.summary_en: A Tibetan Buddhist parable tells of the bodhisattva Jataka in a previous life, born as a prince who renounced his palace to seek enlightenment in the forest. He came upon a mother tiger, starving and too weak to hunt, watching her newborn cubs dying of hunger beside her. The prince saw that the tiger would eat her own cubs before dawn unless she had food. He said to himself: 'I have been seeking to sacrifice the self for ten thousand lives. This moment is here.' He threw himself from the cliff in front of the tiger. She fed on him. The cubs survived. His merit from this act, according to Buddhist teaching, propelled his soul toward final enlightenment. The act of self-offering — dana paramita — is the first of the perfections the bodhisattva must master. The site of this sacrifice is said to be near Taxila, and pilgrims still mark the spot.
stories.summary_zh: 一则藏传佛教本生故事讲述了菩萨的前世：他生为王子，舍弃宫殿，入林求道。行至深处，见一母虎，饥瘦力竭，无力觅食，身旁数只幼虎濒临饿毙。王子知晓，若无食物，母虎天亮前便会吞噬自己的幼崽。他默然告诉自己："我已历一万世，只求以舍弃自我积累功德。此刻，便是了。"他从峭壁跳下，落于虎前。母虎得食，幼虎得救。依照佛教义理，此举的无量功德推动他的灵魂向最终觉悟大步迈进。布施波罗蜜——慷慨的圆满——正是菩萨必须修持的第一项完美品德。据说此地临近塔克西拉，至今仍有朝圣者前往标记这片圣土。

stories.title_en: The Foolish Old Man Who Moved Mountains
stories.summary_en: An old man of ninety, whom everyone called the Foolish Old Man, lived in a valley blocked by two great mountains. The long way around exhausted everyone. He announced he would move the mountains. His neighbor laughed: 'You are an old man with few years left. Even if you began today, you could not move a single mountain in your lifetime, let alone two.' The Foolish Old Man replied: 'When I die, my sons will continue. When they die, my grandsons will continue. Mountains cannot grow taller, but my descendants go on forever. In the end the mountains must yield.' He and his sons began digging every day, carrying the earth to the distant sea. Heaven heard of his persistence and was moved. The Emperor of Heaven sent two divine giants who carried the two mountains away on their backs and set them elsewhere. This story, popularized by Mao Zedong as a political parable, entered Chinese cultural consciousness as the definition of determined perseverance.
stories.summary_zh: 传说有位名叫愚公的九旬老翁，世居两山之间，山势阻隔，出行绕道劳顿难当。他宣布要移走这两座山。邻居嘲笑道："你风烛残年，就算从今日起动工，一辈子也移不了一座山，何况两座？"愚公答曰："我死后，有儿子；儿子死了，有孙子；子子孙孙，无穷匮也。山不会长高，但我的后代永无止境，终有一天，山终究要让路。"他带着儿子们日日挖山，将土石运往遥远的海边。天帝为其坚持所感动，派来两位神将，将两座大山背走，分置他处。此故事后经毛泽东引申为政治寓言，由此深入中国人的文化意识，成为坚韧不拔精神的代名词。

stories.title_en: The Story of Oni and the Bean-Throwing Festival
stories.summary_en: In Japanese tradition, Oni are demons of the supernatural world — great, fearsome creatures with wild hair, fanged mouths, iron clubs, and skin of red, blue, or green. They come from Jigoku, the underworld, and carry away the souls of the wicked. But on the night of Setsubun — the eve of the first day of spring — the boundary between the demon world and the human world weakens, and Oni slip through. Families drive them back with roasted soybeans, throwing them at the door or at a family member wearing an Oni mask, shouting 'Oni wa soto! Fuku wa uchi!' — 'Demons out! Fortune in!' The beans are connected to a play on the Japanese word for demon-destroying. In some older regions, the Oni are not entirely evil — they are also the forces of untamed nature, necessary and wild, who must be acknowledged and propitiated at the year's turning.
stories.summary_zh: 在日本传统中，鬼是超自然世界的恶魔——形体庞大，令人生畏，毛发蓬乱，獠牙张露，手持铁棒，皮肤或红或蓝或绿。它们来自地狱（Jigoku），负责摄取恶人之魂。然而在节分之夜——立春前夕——人间与鬼界的边界变得脆弱，鬼趁机溜入人间。家家户户以炒熟的黄豆驱鬼：向门外抛撒，或朝戴着鬼面具的家人投掷，同时高喊：「鬼は外！福は内！」（鬼出去！福进来！）黄豆的使用与日语中"驱鬼"的谐音密切相关。在某些较古老的地区，鬼并非纯粹的邪恶之物——它们也是狂野自然力量的化身，必要而不可或缺，是在年岁更迭之际必须加以正视与安抚的存在。

stories.title_en: The River God and the Beautiful Bride
stories.summary_en: In the state of Wei during the Warring States period, the corrupt local officials and witch-priests of Ye County had devised a scheme: each year they declared that the River God Hebo required a beautiful girl as his bride or he would send floods. The priests selected a girl from a poor family, dressed her elaborately, paraded her through the city, and cast her into the Yellow River. The city paid enormous 'offerings' to the priests who pocketed the money. When the brilliant official Ximen Bao was appointed to govern the county, he listened to the story and said nothing — until the day of the next 'wedding.' He attended the ceremony, inspected the girl, and declared she was not beautiful enough for the River God. He ordered the head priest thrown into the river to deliver this message. When the priest did not return, he threw in the chief disciples. Then he said: 'The River God seems to be entertaining guests. We will wait.' The ceremony was never held again.
stories.summary_zh: 战国时期魏国邺县，贪腐官员与巫祝勾结，每年以"河伯娶妻"之名，将穷苦人家的女儿盛妆打扮、游街示众，投入黄河，向民间索取巨额"祭品"中饱私囊。智者西门豹被委派治理邺县，听闻此事，不动声色，只待祭河之日亲赴现场。他端详那名被选定的女子，正色道：此女不够美貌，不足以配河伯，须请大巫代为禀报。随即命人将大巫投入河中。见巫者迟迟未归，又将几名弟子相继投入，而后若无其事道："河伯宾客似乎正在款待，我们稍候片刻。"此后，"河伯娶妻"的陋习再未出现。

stories.title_en: The Tanuki and the Tea Kettle
stories.summary_en: A Buddhist monk purchased a seemingly ordinary iron tea kettle at a market. That night, as he began to heat it over the fire, something strange happened: the kettle began to grow a face, sprout fur, and develop a tail. It was a tanuki — a magical raccoon dog — that had transformed itself into a kettle to escape a cold winter night. The startled monk sold it to a rag-and-bone man, who discovered the tanuki could also walk a tightrope and perform acrobatics. They traveled from town to town putting on shows, splitting the profits. The tanuki grew fat and happy. When winter passed, the tanuki asked to return to the temple, but the monk refused. The rag-man, however, had grown fond of the creature and let it come and go as it pleased. The kettle still sits in the temple of Morinji today, identified as a national treasure.
stories.summary_zh: 一位禅僧在集市买来一只看似普通的铁茶壶。当夜，他将茶壶架于火上加热，奇事发生了：茶壶长出了面孔、皮毛与尾巴——原来是一只为躲避严冬而化身茶壶的狸猫（貉）。惊慌失措的禅僧将其卖给一位收废品的商人，商人发现狸猫还能走钢丝、表演杂耍，便带着它走遍各地卖艺，分账而居，狸猫吃得圆滚滚，过得十分滋润。冬去春来，狸猫请求回归寺院，禅僧拒绝。而那商人已对这小生灵生了情分，任其自由往来。那口茶壶至今仍供奉于茂林寺，被列为日本国宝。

stories.title_en: The Two Brothers and the Tide Jewels
stories.title_zh: 山幸彦与海幸彦·潮汐宝珠
stories.summary_en: In ancient Japan, two brothers — Hoori the mountain hunter and Hoderi the sea fisher — exchanged tools for a day. Hoori lost his brother's prized fishing hook in the sea. Desperate, he dove to the Dragon Palace of the Sea God, Watatsumi. There he married the sea god's daughter, Toyotama-hime, and lived happily for three years, forgetting the hook entirely. His bride finally found the hook for him in the throat of a bream fish. The Sea God also gave him two magical jewels — one that could raise the tide and one that could lower it. Hoori returned to the surface. His brother Hoderi, still angry about the hook, attacked him. Hoori raised the tide jewel; Hoderi begged for mercy. He lowered the tide jewel. Hoderi submitted. Their descendants included Jimmu Tenno — the legendary first Emperor of Japan.
stories.summary_zh: 远古日本，兄弟二人——山幸彦（火远理命）与海幸彦（火照命）——互换工具各试一日。山幸彦不慎将兄长的珍贵鱼钩沉入海底，情急之下潜入海神渡都美（Watatsumi）的龙宫。他与海神之女丰玉姬相爱成婚，在水晶宫中安居三年，竟将鱼钩之事抛于脑后。爱妻终于在一条鲷鱼的喉咙里寻回鱼钩，海神又赠予他两枚神奇宝珠——一可涨潮，一可退潮。山幸彦重返地面，兄长海幸彦仍怀恨在心，前来寻衅。山幸彦祭出涨潮珠，海浪奔涌，兄长求饶不已，随即以退潮珠将其平息。海幸彦俯首称臣。二人的后裔中，便有日本传说中的第一位天皇——神武天皇。
